content.problemStatement:
All strings in Chefland are beautiful because they are binary strings (a binary string contains only characters '0' and '1'). The beauty of a binary string SSS is defined as the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) (1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§‚ą£S‚ą£1 \le i \le j \le |S|1‚Č§i‚Č§j‚Č§‚ą£S‚ą£) such that the substring Si,Si+1,‚Ä¶,SjS_i, S_{i+1}, \ldots, S_jSi‚Äč,Si+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Sj‚Äč is special.

For a binary string UUU, let's denote the number of occurrences of the characters '1' and '0' in UUU by cnt1cnt_1cnt1‚Äč and cnt0cnt_0cnt0‚Äč respectively; then, UUU is special if cnt0=cnt1‚čÖcnt1cnt_0 = cnt_1 \cdot cnt_1cnt0‚Äč=cnt1‚Äč‚čÖcnt1‚Äč.

Today, Chef's friend Araspa is celebrating her birthday. Chef wants to give Araspa the most beautiful binary string he can find. Currently, he is checking out binary strings in a shop, but he needs your help to calculate their beauties. Tell Chef the beauty of each binary string he gives you.
constraints: each character of SSS is '0' or '1'
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the beauty of the string SSS.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The special substrings correspond to (i,j)=(1,2),(1,6),(2,3),(5,6)(i, j) = (1, 2), (1, 6), (2, 3), (5, 6)(i,j)=(1,2),(1,6),(2,3),(5,6).
